channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ACT Government to Enforce Parking Rules at Local Sports Grounds Amid Facility Concerns
item.description: Canberra parents and sports clubs are facing a tough weekend as the ACT government launches a targeted parking enforcement blitz at major local sports grounds. Grounds such as Kaleen, Harrison, O’Conn...

item.title: Increasing Numbers of Canadian Jews Relocate Abroad Amid Rising Antisemitism
item.description: In recent years, a growing number of Canadian Jews have been leaving the country, primarily relocating to Israel and the United States, driven by concerns about rising antisemitism. According to the I...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain and Strong Winds Across Several Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather forecast for the next 24 hours, highlighting scattered rainfall across multiple counties in Kenya. The heaviest showers are expected over...

item.title: 84-Year-Old Man Missing in Tampines Found Safe by Singapore Police
item.description: Singapore police announced on May 16, 2026, that an 84-year-old man reported missing the previous day has been located and is safe. Mr. U Swee, also known as Chang Yann Ching, was last seen around 2 p...

item.title: Bandi Sai Bhageerath, son of Union minister, surrenders for questioning in Pocso case
item.description: Bandi Sai Bhageerath, who is the son of a Union minister, surrendered before the police on Sunday following the issuance of a look-out circular against him in connection with a case under the Protecti...

item.title: B.C. Tribunal Dismisses Claim That Classical Liberal Beliefs Are Protected Political Views
item.description: A recent decision by the British Columbia Human Rights Tribunal dismissed a complaint from former political science professor Joshua Gordon, who alleged he was denied a tenure-track position at Simon ...

item.title: CBSE Implements Three-Language Policy for Classes 9 and 10 with Internal Assessment for Third Language
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has officially rolled out the three-language policy for students from Class 9 onwards, starting July 1, 2026. Under this policy, students must study thr...

item.title: Trump's Key Foreign Policy Goals Face Setbacks Amid Economic and Geopolitical Challenges
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's second term is facing a series of setbacks, with his ambitious foreign policy goals encountering major obstacles. His recent trip to China concluded without any substanti...

item.title: India Successfully Repatriates 11th Century Chola Copper Plates from Netherlands
item.description: In a significant development for India's cultural heritage, the Netherlands has formally returned the ancient Chola Copper Plates, also known as the Leiden Plates, to India. Prime Minister Narendra Mo...

item.title: Centre alleges Oxfam India breached FCRA norms and campaigned against Assam tea industry
item.description: The Union government has informed the Delhi High Court that Oxfam India, a non-governmental organisation, was involved in activities harmful to the country’s economic interests and violated the Foreig...

item.title: Carney and Smith Reach Agreement on Industrial Carbon Pricing Amid Alberta Tensions
item.description: On May 15, 2026, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith signed an implementation agreement under the Canada-Alberta memorandum of understanding. The eight-page agreement address...

item.title: Eurovision 2026 Grand Final: Australia and the UK Compete Among Favourites in Vienna
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest 2026 grand final is set to take place in Vienna, Austria, with millions of fans across Europe and Australia tuning in to see which country will claim the coveted trophy. Au...

item.title: Astronomers Capture the Clearest Image of a Cosmic Web Filament Connecting Galaxies
item.description: Astronomers have captured the sharpest direct image ever of a filament in the cosmic web, a massive network of matter that connects galaxies across the Universe. This glowing strand spans roughly 3 mi...

item.title: Mixed Reactions as State House Nairobi Gets Modern Renovation
item.description: The newly renovated State House in Nairobi has sparked heated debate among Kenyans. While some citizens and social media users are unhappy with the redesign, pointing out that it resembles President W...

item.title: Minister Explains High Failure Rate in CSS Exams Due to Poor Preparation and Tough Competition
item.description: Minister-in-Charge of the Establishment Division Senator Ahad Khan Cheema has stated that the high failure rate in Central Superior Services (CSS) examinations is mainly because of weak academic prepa...

item.title: John Lennon and Yoko Ono’s Historic Wedding in Gibraltar
item.description: On March 20, 1969, John Lennon and Yoko Ono were married in Gibraltar, marking one of the most unconventional and iconic weddings in pop culture history. Initially, the couple wanted to marry on a cro...

item.title: Nigerian Court Jails Social Media User for Spreading False Death Rumour About Adekunle Gold and Simi’s Daughter
item.description: One Nigerian court don sentence one social media user wey dem know as Samuel Adeboye Adeyeye, popularly called Swanky_concept on X, to two years imprisonment. The guy plead guilty to spreading false i...

item.title: George Rodriguez’s 1978 Portraits Capture Young Janet Jackson’s Early Career
item.description: In 1978, legendary Chicano photographer George Rodriguez captured intimate portraits of a 12-year-old Janet Jackson, providing a rare glimpse into the early life of the future pop superstar. At this t...

item.title: How Motivational Quotes Helped Me Heal After Divorce
item.description: After nearly three decades of marriage, Stephanie Vuckovic found herself devastated when her husband left, stating they were 'better off as friends.' Struggling with grief and loss, she turned to soci...

item.title: Toronto Police Report Highlights Disproportionate Impact of Hate Crimes on Jewish Community in 2025
item.description: In 2025, Jews in Toronto were disproportionately affected by religion-motivated hate crimes, representing 82% of all such incidents according to the Toronto Police Service. Anti-Muslim crimes accounte...
